coaching.coaching: Pin down curl credential exposure in OS process table with a concrete worked example.
coaching.subject: curl credential exposure in OS process table

coaching.coaching: Realign credential guidance scope vs parent epic boundary to the parent's plan — the parent wins on conflict; if the parent is genuinely wrong, update the PARENT first (which forces its re-review), never silently diverge the leaf.
coaching.subject: credential guidance scope vs parent epic boundary

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.finding: The leaf's declared scope — adding Gerrit REST credential-helper guidance to AGENTS.md/CONTRIBUTING.md — is entirely outside the parent epic's scope, which is limited to plan-review attestation infrastructure (signed pins, plan_review_phase projection, execution-review compatibility, close gates, prerequisite consistency, derived health). The parent's plan, 22 ACs, and 'Not in scope' list contain no mention of Gerrit credentials, curl --netrc, git credential fill, non-interactive Codex authentication, or any contributor documentation deliverable.
findings.location: Leaf plan (Goal, Plan, Acceptance Criteria) vs. parent jira-reb-1160 scope and ACs
findings.suggested_fix: Create a new standalone task or story (or find an existing contributor-docs epic) that covers 'Improve Codex contributor guidance for non-interactive Gerrit authentication' and re-parent this leaf under it via `rebar link be4e-8716-87bf-4dd0 <new-parent-id> parent`. If no suitable parent exists, this leaf can be promoted to a standalone task with no parent rather than remaining incorrectly parented under jira-reb-1160.

findings.finding: The ticket has no `## Acceptance Criteria` checklist. A plan cannot be reviewed for completion without testable, checkable criteria.
findings.suggested_fix: Add an `## Acceptance Criteria` section with `- [ ]` checklist items, one per observable, in-session-verifiable outcome.

findings.finding: AC3 states 'Documentation checks pass' but the plan never names which checks cover the new credential-helper/netrc/401 content. The only existing Codex-guidance test (tests/unit/test_codex_agent_guidance.py) asserts a fixed set of strings ('feature-branch-driver', 'LLM-Review +1', etc.) that do not include 'git credential fill', 'netrc', '/a/', or any 401-explanation text. Whether the new AC1/AC2 content will be gated by a new or updated test is left entirely implicit.

tests/unit/test_codex_agent_guidance.py is a direct consumer of the **Codex Gerrit workflow rule:** block in AGENTS.md that the plan proposes to extend with credential-helper REST content, but the plan does not enumerate this file as an edit target or acknowledge that its assertions may need updating to cover the new guidance.
findings.location: Plan section: Plan body / AC1; file: tests/unit/test_codex_agent_guidance.py
findings.suggested_fix: Add a second test function (or extend the existing one) in test_codex_agent_guidance.py that asserts `'git credential fill'` and a key phrase distinguishing the netrc-401 explanation (e.g. `'curl --netrc'` or `'--netrc 401'`) appear in the AGENTS.md Codex section.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify in the AGENTS.md shell pattern that the password must NOT appear on the curl command line (e.g., use `--netrc-file <(printf 'machine %s login %s password %s' "$host" "$user" "$pass")` or `curl -K -` reading a config from stdin) to prevent process-table leakage, and add a corresponding assertion in the test.
evidence: 'passes them directly to curl, and never echoes or logs the password' — this constraint covers log-channel leakage but is silent on process-table leakage
ABSENCE: the plan names no curl mechanism (--netrc-file, -K -, --config, process substitution) that avoids putting the password on the command line
Common shell pattern `curl -u "$user:$pass"` satisfies no-echo but exposes the credential in /proc/<pid>/cmdline and `ps` output on Linux hosts
findings.finding: The plan mandates 'never echoes or logs the password' but does not specify that the curl invocation must avoid exposing the password in the OS process table. A typical shell pattern (`curl -u "$user:$pass"`) satisfies the no-echo constraint yet makes the password visible to any co-tenant process via `ps aux`, because command-line arguments are world-readable in /proc on Linux. The plan's Implementation section says 'passes them directly to curl' without constraining the mechanism, leaving agents likely to use the `-u user:pass` flag.
findings.location: Implementation section — 'Provide a shell pattern that keeps credentials in variables, passes them directly to curl, and never echoes or logs the password'; AC line 3 — 'The documented example never prints or logs the password'
scenarios: A Codex agent follows the documented pattern using `curl -u "$user:$pass"`, satisfying the 'no echo/log' requirement, but any process on the same host can read the password from /proc during the request.
A shared CI runner or developer machine with multiple users exposes Gerrit credentials to co-tenant processes for the lifetime of the curl call.
findings.suggested_fix:
In the documented shell pattern, use a mechanism that avoids putting the password in argv. For example:
```sh
creds=$(git credential fill <<EOF
protocol=https
host=<gerrit-host>
EOF
)
user=$(printf '%s' "$creds" | grep '^username=' | cut -d= -f2)
pass=$(printf '%s' "$creds" | grep '^password=' | cut -d= -f2)
curl --silent --netrc-file <(printf 'machine %s login %s password %s\n' "<gerrit-host>" "$user" "$pass") \
  "https://<gerrit-host>/a/..."
```
This satisfies no-echo AND prevents process-table exposure. Add a test assertion for the absence of `-u` (or assert for `--netrc-file` / `-K`) alongside the existing no-echo check.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
